div.h1: Latency Distribution — Why P99 Matters More Than Mean

div.p:
Key Insight: Mean latency of 287ns suggests your stack is fast.
But P99 of 6,240ns means 1% of packets are 22× slower.
In HFT, that 1% is where races are won and lost.

g.text: 64-128ns | 87% | All L1 cache hits, no contention
256-512ns | 2% | L3 cache hit or prefetch miss
Optimization Target | Eliminate slow path entirely
Prefetch packet data while parsing headers
Eliminate ALL locks from data path
Allocate buffers on NIC's NUMA node
Pad structures to cache line boundaries
Profile P99, not mean | perf record -g

text.tspan: // In HFT, you don't compete with the
// One slow packet = one lost trade.
// In HFT, you don't compete with the
// One slow packet = one lost trade.
// On P99 packets, you arrive 4.2μs later
